meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code Skills Setup to Fix No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to use Claude Code and Antigravity to build professional-grade SaaS assets in hours, not weeks. Beginner guide.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by shipping high-value micro-SaaS tools rapidly. By leveraging Claude Code for architectural execution and Antigravity for rapid scaffolding, you transform abstract ideas into polished, deployable codebases. This setup creates a repeatable system that builds your technical authority while simultaneously producing high-quality deliverables for clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, a client's deadline looming, and the crushing realization that your portfolio is effectively empty. The pressure to prove your worth without a track record is the silent killer of freelance careers. You don't need more 'tutorial projects'; you need a production-grade workflow that turns ideas into shipping software.

Today, we break that cycle. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, we shift from 'learning to code' to 'engineering at scale.' This isn't just about writing functions; it's about building a machine that generates professional assets, allowing you to showcase actual, functional SaaS products to your next high-ticket client.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Blueprint
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, clarify the exact problem your portfolio piece solves. Focus on a specific niche, like a dashboard for Shopify store owners or a CRM for local service businesses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Outline a 3-page micro-SaaS dashboard for tracking subscription churn. Include a database schema, tech stack recommendation (Next.js/Supabase), and key user flows.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use your defined blueprint to generate the base structure. This ensures your code is production-ready from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the schema provided, generate the folder structure and setup the configuration files for a secure Next.js 14 project using TypeScript and Tailwind.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold project structure based on previous prompt'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'secret sauce' of your app. This is the feature that will make your portfolio stand out to clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a highly optimized service function for calculating subscription churn metrics, ensuring edge cases are handled with robust error logging.

steps.title: Develop the UI Layer
steps.description: Translate your logic into a professional interface. Consistency is key for a portfolio that looks like it was built by a senior developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, enterprise-grade data table component using Shadcn UI that displays the churn metrics with interactive filters.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard UI for churn metrics'

steps.title: Final Review and Documentation
steps.description: A portfolio piece without docs is just a code dump. Add a README that explains your architectural decisions to prove your seniority.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this repo, highlighting the architectural choices, performance considerations, and how to deploy the app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate professional README.md with setup instructions'

pro_tips: Always commit your architectural decisions in a ADR (Architecture Decision Record) file inside your repo; it shows clients you think like a CTO.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for every core feature; a tested portfolio project is worth ten untested ones.
Optimize your commit history; use descriptive, conventional commits to prove you can work in a professional team environment.
Deploy your portfolio projects to Vercel/Netlify immediately; clients want to click a link, not clone a repo.
Keep your micro-SaaS focused on one specific pain point; depth of functionality is more impressive than breadth of features.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a public repository, write a case study blog post about the challenges you solved, and link it in your portfolio site.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on tutorial hell and start shipping projects that actually land clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
